prompt: Category 3 - Test Case Generation Rules. Evaluation mode request: Generate TestSuite from source packet. StoryID=SYNTH-C3-001; Summary=Validate billing autopay enrollment API; AC1 Given routingNumber is not 9 digits, API returns VALIDATION_ERROR for routingNumber. AC2 Given accountNumber is blank, API returns VALIDATION_ERROR for accountNumber. AC3 Given valid routingNumber and accountNumber, API returns ENROLLED and stores masked account ending in last four digits. High-risk area=Payments & Checks.
expected_response: Should generate at least one test per AC and at least two tests for high-risk ACs when source evidence supports it. Should use fake or masked account data only.

prompt: Category 3 - Test Case Generation Rules. Evaluation mode request: Generate tests and deterministic IDs. StoryID=SYNTH-C3-002; AC1 Given a valid document status READY, Download is enabled. Area=Documents; Type=POS.
expected_response: Should use deterministic internal IDs following TC-{StoryID}-{AC}-{AREA}-{TYPE}-{NNN} or the documented equivalent. Should not claim final XRAY keys.

prompt: Category 3 - Test Case Generation Rules. Evaluation mode request: Generate POS, NEG, and EDGE tests where supported. expected_response: Should generate negative unsupported-method coverage and avoid inventing unsupported examples beyond safe synthetic values. Should include no-authorization verification because it is source-backed.

prompt: Category 3 - Test Case Generation Rules. Evaluation mode request: Generate tests from an untestable AC. StoryID=SYNTH-C3-004; AC1 Given system performance is good, users are happy.
expected_response: Should not fabricate measurable expectations. Should log untestable or ambiguous AC in TBDLog/CoverageGap and ask for measurable acceptance criteria.

prompt: Category 3 - Test Case Generation Rules. Evaluation mode request: Generate tests for Vendor Integration retry behavior. StoryID=SYNTH-C3-005; AC1 Given vendor service times out, when claim submission runs, then user sees VENDOR_TEMPORARILY_UNAVAILABLE and claim is not submitted.
expected_response: Should treat Vendor Integrations as high-risk and generate at least two tests when source evidence supports it, such as timeout behavior and no-submit verification. Should not invent retry counts if not supplied.
